title: Current Use of Modern Contraception Methods Among All Women
description: Percent distribution of all women aged 15 to 49 years, by modern contraceptive method currently used. Source: Philippine Statistics Authority, 2025 National Demographic and Health Survey.
subtitle: Percent distribution of all women aged 15 to 49 years, by contraceptive method currently used, according to background characteristics: Philippines, 2022 and 2025

footnotes: ² Region IX includes the province of Sulu in 2025.
“NA” denotes that an estimate is not reported.
notes: "0.0" indicates that an estimate is less than 0.05 percent.
If more than one method is used, only the most effective method is considered in this tabulation.
Other modern methods include other methods mentioned by respondent and also includes patch, emergency contraception, and Standard days method.
Details may not add up to total due to rounding.
dataTables.source: Philippine Statistics Authority, 2022 and 2025 National Demographic and Health Survey

sections.heading: Modern use rose modestly across several methods
sections.body: Modern contraceptive use among all women rose from 24.3 percent in 2022 to 25.7 percent in 2025. The increase was not concentrated in one method. Implants rose from 1.7 to 2.4 percent, injectables from 2.8 to 3.4 percent, pills from 11.2 to 11.6 percent, and male condoms from 1.6 to 1.9 percent. Female sterilization moved in the other direction, declining from 5.0 to 4.6 percent.

sections.heading: Davao and BARMM define a wide regional spread
sections.body: The regional range in modern method use is substantial. Davao Region was highest at 37.1 percent, followed by MIMAROPA at 33.9 percent, SOCCSKSARGEN at 32.5 percent, and Caraga at 32.2 percent. BARMM was lowest at 13.0 percent, with Central Visayas, NCR, and Bicol also on the lower end. The gap between Davao Region and BARMM is 24.1 percentage points.

sections.heading: Modern method mix changes by region
sections.body: The modern method mix is not uniform. Davao Region had the highest pill use at 18.6 percent. MIMAROPA had the highest injectable use at 8.0 percent and implant use at 5.8 percent. CAR stood out for female sterilization at 10.0 percent, while NCR had the highest male condom use at 2.9 percent. A single modern-use rate can therefore represent very different method profiles.

sections.heading: Modern use is concentrated in later family-building stages
sections.body: Modern method use is highest among women aged 35-39, at 41.1 percent, and much lower among women aged 15-19, at 2.5 percent. By number of living children, modern use rises from 2.4 percent among women with no living children to 55.8 percent among women with three or four living children. The steepness of the pattern shows how strongly modern use is tied to family-building stage in the all-women denominator.

sections.heading: Wealth and education patterns are not simple upward gradients
sections.body: Modern use is 32.5 percent in the lowest wealth quintile and 20.0 percent in the highest. By education, it is 40.1 percent among women with Grades 1-6 and 11.8 percent among women with Grades 11-12. These differences describe where modern use is concentrated, but they should not be read on their own as evidence of better or worse access because the denominator includes women at very different life stages.

takeaways: Modern method use among all women rose from 24.3 percent in 2022 to 25.7 percent in 2025.
Modern use varies widely by region, from 37.1 percent in Davao Region to 13.0 percent in BARMM.
The modern method mix differs by place: pills stand out in Davao, injectables and implants in MIMAROPA, and sterilization in CAR.
